title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: The United States faces a severe aluminum supply crisis, with Bloomberg warning that supplies could hit zero within 30 days due to a 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum. This tariff, intended to protect domestic production, has triggered disruptions threatening American manufacturing. Production issues in the Middle East have eliminated major foreign sources, potentially widening the deficit to 1.5 million tons.

title: US to build largest aluminum plant with 500,000-ton annual capacity
summary: The US is set to build its first aluminum smelter since the 1980s in Inola, Oklahoma, by Century Aluminum, with a capacity of 500,000 tons annually, including high-purity aluminum for defense. The project, supported by a $500 million DOE grant and partnership with Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA), aims to reduce import dependence and create jobs. This marks a strategic shift to onshore critical mineral production.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock Raises Risks For Global Metals Markets
summary: An aluminum supply shock, driven by geopolitical risks, trade policy shifts, and energy prices, is tightening global metals markets. StoneX analyst Natalie Scott-Gray discusses rising disruptions in aluminum and tin supplies alongside weakening copper demand. The outlook highlights diverging investor positioning amid inventory builds.

title: EGA and Century Aluminum partner on US production plant
summary: Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA) and Century Aluminum are partnering on a new US aluminum production plant. This collaboration supports efforts to expand domestic capacity. The news appears alongside reports like Egyptalum doubling its aluminum wire production.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,195 CNY/T on April 30, 2026, up 1.59% from the previous day, with a 2.24% increase over the past month and 3.90% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures approached CNY 3,200 per ton amid strong seasonal demand, restocking, and higher input costs from the Middle East conflict. The World Steel Association projects global steel demand to grow 0.3% this year to 1.724 billion tons and 2.2% next year.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel fell to 1,120.04 USD/T on May 4, 2026, down 1.06% from the previous day, after reaching 1075.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, prices gained 4.99%, reflecting recent upward trends in the market.

title: US manufacturing grew in April
summary: US manufacturing PMI held at 52.7 in April, marking four consecutive months of growth, driven by new orders rising to 54.1 while production eased slightly. The prices index surged to 84.6, the highest since April 2022, fueled by increases in steel and aluminum prices, tariffs, and petroleum products amid the Middle East conflict. Export orders contracted further, signaling challenges in global demand.

title: US Midwest Domestic Hot-Rolled Coil Steel Futures Price Today
summary: US Steel Coil futures are currently priced at 1,084.00, matching the previous close, amid ongoing market activity in flat-rolled steel.

title: AISI: Raw steel production holds near multi-year high
summary: Domestic raw steel production in the US was estimated at 1,830,000 short tons for the week ending April 25, down 1.0% from the prior week but near the multi-year high of 1,848,000 tons set previously. Year-to-date production totals 29,549,000 tons, up 6.0% from 2025, with a capability utilization rate of 79.3%. Production declined in most regions, including Northeast, Great Lakes, South, and West.

title: United States Steel Corporation announced a US$1.9 billion investment to construct a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at Big River Steel
summary: United States Steel Corporation announced a $1.9 billion investment for a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at Big River Steel. This expansion aims to boost steel production capacity. The announcement highlights ongoing efforts to enhance domestic steelmaking infrastructure.

title: Pennsylvania Steelmaker Plans Environmental Upgrades
summary: Re-roller Gautier Steel has been awarded a US$1.9 million grant for upgrades that will lower carbon emissions and improve energy efficiency. This initiative represents continued investment in sustainable steel production technologies.

title: Free US Steel to invest $1.9 billion to build DRI facility at Big River Steel Works
summary: US Steel is investing $1.9 billion to construct a Direct Reduced Iron (DRI) facility at its Big River Steel Works location. DRI technology represents a lower-emissions alternative to traditional blast furnace steelmaking methods.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices rose to 19,410 USD/T on May 1, 2026, up 0.10% from the previous day, with a 13.51% increase over the past month and 25.79% year-over-year. Futures traded above $19,400, a near two-year high, driven by supply disruptions including mining quota cuts in Indonesia and a sulfur shortage from Strait of Hormuz issues. These factors have constrained ore supply and raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Nickel prices held steady at $19,373.28 per ton globally and ¥132,312 per ton in China, with a 0.19% edge up to $19,127.31/Ton globally on April 28. Bullish momentum stems from Indonesia's slashed mining quotas tightening ore supply and a global sulfur shortage due to Middle East tensions impacting MHP processing. A weaker US dollar and firm SHFE supplier quotes further support the price floor.

title: Nornickel says nickel output flat year-on-year in first quarter
summary: Russia’s Nornickel reported nickel output of 42,000 metric tons in Q1 2026, unchanged from the previous year. The company maintained its full-year forecast at 193,000-203,000 tons. This reflects steady production amid global market dynamics.

title: Nornickel keeps nickel output steady in Q1, cuts copper by 10%
summary: Nornickel held nickel production at 42,000 tons in Q1 2026, matching last year, while copper output dropped 10% to 99,000 tons due to a higher base effect. The company cited operational factors in its statement. Nickel stability contrasts with copper declines.

title: Indonesia nickel processing utilization to drop amid lower nickel ore production quota
summary: Indonesia’s nickel processing capacity utilization is projected to fall to 70-75% in 2026 from 90% last year due to reduced nickel ore quotas. FINI expects surging nickel ore imports to offset domestic constraints. This could impact global supply chains.
